rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectasis in the basal segments bilaterally.  
Numerous nodules of varying density are present in the lung parenchyma; in series 5 the largest / index nodules are as follows:

In the right lung:  
— In the upper lobe:  
In the peripheral regions, peribronchial nodules form a tree-in-bud pattern, 2–5 mm (images 68–99) - not previously present.

In the left lung:  
— In the upper lobe:  
Solid nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 109) - previously 1.0 × 8 mm: closely abuts the segmental bronchus (bronchocele?). Slightly caudal to the above nodule, a similar-appearing nodule measures up to 5 mm (image 105) - previously up to 6 mm.  
* Subsolid nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 102) - no change.  
Solid nodule measuring up to 3 mm (image 94) - not previously present.  
* Solid nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 132) - no change.  
— In the lower lobe:  
* Subsolid nodule measuring up to 9 mm (image 139) - not previously present.

Calcification in the right upper lobe measuring up to 3 mm (image 112). No pleural effusion.  
The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Aortic valve calcification. Mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is dilated up to 4.5 cm (previously 4.4 cm), with wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone density in vertebrae at the T11-T12 level to less than 100 HU (CT signs of osteoporosis) - previously density in the 114-106 HU range. Decreased height of the T7 vertebral body up to 25% - previously less pronounced. Healing fracture of the left 11th rib.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
rad_impression_en:
1. CT findings of multiple pulmonary nodules of varying density:  
- In the right upper lobe, clustered nodules (not previously identified), when correlated with clinical, laboratory, and history data, may correspond to manifestations of bronchiolitis.  
- New isolated subsolid nodules in the left lower lobe: per the 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, consider CT in 3 months, PET/CT, or biopsy sampling.  
- Solid nodules in the left upper lobe, in close proximity to segmental/subsegmental bronchi, with interval decrease in size (bronchocele?).

2. Mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Aortic valve calcification.

4. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta: dilation of the ascending aorta, with mild interval increase in diameter.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation, decreased height of the T7 vertebral body, CT signs of osteoporosis (with interval progression). Healing fracture of the left 11th rib.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectasis in the basal segments bilaterally. In the right upper lobe in posterior segment and at the apex, centrilobular nodules form a tree-in-bud pattern, 2–5 mm (images 68–99), not previously seen.
Numerous nodules of varying density are present in the lung parenchyma; in series 5 the largest nodules are as follows:
In the left upper lobe:  
- Solid nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 109) - previously 1.0 × 0.8 cm: closely abuts the subsegmental bronchus. Slightly caudal to the above nodule, a similar-appearing nodule measures up to 6 mm (image 105), unchanged.  
- Solid nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 43) - not previously seen.  
- Solid nodule measuring up to 3 mm (image 94) - not previously seen.  
- Subsolid nodule measuring up to 6 mm (image 102) - no change.  
- Solid nodule measuring up to 4 mm (image 132) - no change. 
In the left lower lobe:  
- Ground glass nodules measuring up to 7 mm (image 139, 162) - not previously present.
No pleural effusion. The trachea and main bronchi are patent. Wall thickening of the subsegmental and subsegmental bronchi with secretion. 

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly, mild enlargement of the left ventricle. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. Severe aortic valve calcification. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, and left circumflex arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is dilated up to 4.5 cm (previously 4.4 cm), with wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone density in vertebrae at the T11-T12 level to less than 100 HU (CT signs of osteoporosis) - previously density in the 114–106 HU range. Decreased height of the T7 vertebral body up to 25% - previously less pronounced. Healing fracture of the left 11th rib.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression_en:
1. CT findings of multiple pulmonary nodules of varying density:  
- In the right upper lobe, clustered nodules forming tree-in-bud pattern (not previously identified), may correspond to manifestations of bronchiolitis.  
- New ground glass nodules in the left lower lobe may correspond to manifestations of bronchiolitis / small airway disease. 
- New solid nodules in the left lung.  For patients with low risk of lung cancer, initial chest CT at 3-6 months. If stable, no further follow-up or an optional chest CT at 18-24 months if there is high index of suspicion. For patients with high risk of lung cancer, initial chest CT at 3-6 months. If stable, another chest CT at 18-24 months.
- Solid nodules in the left upper lobe, in close proximity to subsegmental bronchi, with interval decrease in size, may be a bronchocele or inflammatory nodules. 

2. Wall thickening of the subsegmental and subsegmental bronchi with secretion may represent inflammatory bronchitis.

3. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, and left circumflex arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Dilation of the ascending aorta, with mild interval increase in diameter. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta. Severe aortic valve calcification.


DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Linear atelectasis in the right middle lobe, lingular segments, and in lung bases on the left.  
In the parenchyma of both lungs, there are multiple calcifications measuring up to 2 mm and up to 6 mm (round and linear).  
No solid nodules, subsolid nodules, or ground-glass nodules are identified in the lungs. 1.  
No pleural effusion. Localized thickening of the left costal pleura at the level of the posterior segment of the 10th rib, up to 5 mm in thickness, over a length of about 3.4 cm.  
The trachea and main bronchi are patent.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. Pericardial fluid measures up to 5 mm in thickness. No coronary calcification is identified. Aortic valve calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone density in the T12 vertebra to +146 HU. Healing fracture of the posterior segment of the right 9th rib.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
rad_impression_en:
1. CT appearance of multiple pulmonary calcifications (may represent sequelae of prior inflammatory changes). No nodules requiring further follow-up are identified in the lungs per the 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations. CT signs of a localized area of costal pleural thickening in the left lower lobe (changes may also be postinflammatory).

2. Aortic valve calcification. Small pericardial effusion.

3. Thoracic aorta calcification.

4. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Decreased bone density at T12. Healing fracture of the right 9th rib.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: In the parenchyma of both lungs, there are multiple calcifications measuring up to 6 mm (round and linear) on images 35, 79, 92, 119, 129, 134, 136. A few solid micronodules 1-2 mm in the left lower lobe. No pleural effusion. The trachea and main bronchi are patent. Minimal bronchial wall thickening of subsegmental bronchi may represent bronchitis.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy is identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly, mild enlargement of the left ventricle. Coronary artery calcification is identified in the right coronary artery (image 135), qualitatively assessed as mild. Mild aortic valve calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated, with mild wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with osteophyte formation. Healed fracture of the right 9th rib.

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression_en:
1. Multiple bilateral calcified nodules may represent sequelae of prior inflammatory changes. A few solid micronodules 1-2 mm in the left lower lobe. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Coronary artery calcification is identified in the right coronary artery, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Mild aortic valve calcification. Mild thoracic aorta calcification. Mild enlargement of the left ventricle.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
The study is performed without full inspiration and adequate breath-holding.

Lungs and pleura:
Mosaic attenuation of the lungs. Artifacts from suboptimal breathing for scanning. Against this background: In the left upper lobe, a ground-glass opacity area is present, likely due to suboptimal breathing, and linear atelectasis; no nodules requiring follow-up are identified; a few calcified micronodules measure up to 3 mm.
In the left lower lobe, there is an extensive area of consolidation with preserved bronchial lumina within it (series 5, images 102-155).
A few areas of centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema.
On the left, minimal pleural effusion, including along the interlobar pleura.
Dependent changes in lung bases.
The ground-glass opacity areas seen on the 2019 study are not identified.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No masses are identified. A single enlarged lower paratracheal lymph node measures up to 1.2 cm in short axis (series 4, image 72), with no change.

Heart: Cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Moderate coronary artery calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is dilated to 3.0 cm. Multiple calcifications of the aortic arch and aortic valve.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Osteoarthritis of the shoulder joints (no change).

Upper abdomen: Cholecystectomy.
rad_impression_en:
1. Extensive consolidation in the left lower lobe, inflammatory in nature.

2. Minimal left pleural effusion.

3. Mild emphysema, with no change.

4. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.0 cm) and cardiomegaly, which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

5. A single enlarged lower paratracheal lymph node measuring up to 1.2 cm, with no change.

6. Moderate coronary calcification and aortic calcification, and aortic valve calcification, with no change.

7. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine and osteoarthritis of the shoulder joints, with no change.

8. The ground-glass opacity area(s) seen on the 2019 study are not identified.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: The membranous portion of the trachea prolapses into the lumen (incomplete inspiration). Pronounced respiratory artifacts and pronounced gravitational changes are present. In the left upper lobe, a large ground-glass area is present. In the left lower lobe, an extensive area of consolidation with preserved bronchial lumina is present (series 5, images 102-155). In the right lung, in the lower and middle lobes, subsegmental atelectasis is present. Previously seen cysts in both lungs remain. In the left upper lobe, a calcified nodule measures 5 mm, unchanged. In the left lung, a few calcified nodules measure up to 3 mm. No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. The ground-glass opacity areas seen on the 2019 CT are not identified. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No masses or adenopathy is identified.

Heart: Cardiomegaly, cardiothoracic ratio is 0.56. Pericardial effusion with thickness up to 7 mm. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left anterior descending and left circumflex, qualitatively assessed as moderate. Aortic and mitral valve calcifications.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The left pulmonary artery is dilated up to 3.2 cm, the right up to 2.8 cm. Multiple calcifications of the aortic arch.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Osteoarthritis of the shoulder joints (no change).

Upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression_en:
1. Against the background of incomplete inspiration, respiratory artifacts, and gravitational changes, consolidation and ground-glass opacity of the left lung may be a consequence of an unexpanded lung or, less likely, reflect inflammatory changes.

2. Multiple calcified nodules are likely healed granulomas and require no follow-up.

3. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left anterior descending and left circumflex, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Mild cardiomegaly. Small pericardial effusion. Aortic and mitral valve calcifications. Thoracic aorta calcifications.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Micro-nodules in the right upper lobe measuring up to 2 mm in diameter. Mild ground-glass opacities in both lungs, interlobular septal thickening with formation of a mosaic attenuation pattern. Discoid atelectasis in the anterior and lung bases of the right lung and in the anterior portions of the left lung. Bronchial walls are irregularly thickened.  
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Aortic valve calcification. Left coronary artery stenting.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. S-shaped tortuosity of the thoracic aorta. Dilated main pulmonary artery measuring up to 30 mm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 106 HU, T12 - 91 HU. Decreased height of the L1 vertebral body by 25%.

Upper abdomen: Hiatal hernia measuring 3.1 × 3.4 × 4.1 cm, hernia contents include part of the stomach.
rad_impression_en:
1. Micro-nodules in the right upper lobe. No nodules requiring follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Discoid atelectasis in both lungs.

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as mild. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Aortic valve calcification.

4. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.0 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

5. S-shaped tortuosity of the thoracic aorta.

6. Decreased vertebral body density (CT signs of demineralization).

7. Decreased height of the L1 vertebral body.

8. Hiatal hernia.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: Diffuse mosaic attenuation pattern in both lungs, interlobular septal thickening in the apices of the lungs. Micro-nodules in the right upper lobe measure up to 2 mm in diameter. Discoid atelectasis in the right middle lobe, left lingula, lung bases of the right lung. No signs of interstitial disease (honeycombing, traction bronchiectasis, reticulations) were detected. Mild bronchial wall thickening. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass and no lymphadenopathy. Hiatal hernia measuring 3.1 × 3.4 × 4.1 cm, hernia contents include stomach cardia.

Heart: The heart is slightly enlarged due to increased size of the chambers. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary artery calcifications are identified in the left main, left anterior descending, left circumflex arteries, qualitatively assessed as moderate. Aortic and mitral valve calcifications.

Great vessels: Thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. S-shaped tortuosity of the thoracic aorta. Dilated main pulmonary artery measures up to 30 mm.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 106 HU, T12 - 91 HU. Hemangioma L1 measures up to 2.0 cm, no follow-up is required.

Thyroid: In the right lobe there is a hypodense nodule up to 4 mm. ACR White Paper recommendations (Hoang, et al. J Am Coll Radiol 2015;12:143-150) suggest the following: No need for further follow-up.
val_impression_en:
1. Diffuse mosaic attenuation pattern in both lungs may be consistent with obliterative small airways disease or vascular disease, including pulmonary hypertension. No signs of interstitial disease were detected. Micronodules in the right upper lobe. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Coronary calcification, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Note: Recommendation applies unless the patient has known CAD, prior CABG/stent, trauma indications or intraoperative CTs.

3. Dilated main pulmonary artery (3.0 cm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

4. Aortic and mitral valve calcifications.

5. Decreased vertebral body density (CT signs of demineralization).

6. Hiatal hernia.

rad_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. Lungs are unremarkable.  
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass lesions or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly is identified. No significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification is identified.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Unremarkable.

Upper abdomen: Decreased liver density to +39 HU (previously +28 HU). Bariatric gastric surgery. Metallic clips are visualized in the region of the gastric fundus and the cardioesophageal junction.
rad_impression_en:
1. No nodules requiring follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

2. Diffuse decreased density of the liver parenchyma (previously more pronounced, to +28 HU; currently to +39 HU), which may correspond to hepatic steatosis with positive interval change. Clinical correlation is recommended.

3. Bariatric gastric surgery.
val_findings_en:
Lungs and pleura: In the lung parenchyma, solid nodules are identified, in series 7 presented as follows:
In the upper right lobe:
- 3 mm nodule on image 60.
In the middle right lobe:
- 2 mm nodule on image 95.
In the upper left lobe:
- 3 mm nodule on image 72.
In the lower left lobe:
- 2 mm calcified nodule on image 44, healed granuloma, requires no follow-up.
A few areas of air trapping, clinically insignificant. No consolidation, atelectasis, emphysema, or honeycombing. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and pulmonary hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy. Residual thymus tissue.

Heart: No cardiomegaly is identified. No significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification is identified.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Ossifications along the periphery of the humeral heads are present (the shoulder joints are not fully included in the scanning area), which is consistent with the shoulders radiograph 2/20/2026.

Upper abdomen: Decreased liver density to +39 HU (previously +28 HU), interval improvement of the hepatic steatosis. Gastric surgery, metallic clips are visualized in the region of the gastric fundus and the cardioesophageal junction.
val_impression_en:
1. No consolidation, atelectasis, emphysema, or honeycombing in both lungs. A few nodules up to 3 mm in both lungs. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. No coronary calcification is identified.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.
